title: US and Iran seal peace agreement to end conflict
summary: The pact includes the reopening of the Strait of Hormuz and is received with reservations by some governments.

summary: Japanese Prime Minister Takaichi holds bilateral meetings with leaders of Italy, the United Kingdom and others, and debates the regulation of artificial intelligence.

title: Stock markets soar on prospect of peace in Middle East
summary: Nikkei closes above 69,000 points for the first time in its history, and the Dow Jones rises more than 600 points.

title: World Cup news: Kubo injury, Sweden victory and story of a Cape Verde player
summary: Japan defines its squad for 2026 while several stories surround the World Cup.

title: Japan earns valuable draw against Netherlands in World Cup debut
summary: The Japanese team comes back twice to tie 2-2 and earn a point in an exciting match.

title: Shimotsuma mayor found dead in drainage ditch
summary: The mayor's body was found in a sewer, causing shock in the city of Ibaraki.

title: Japan promotes cybersecurity measures with AI as controversies arise
summary: The government gathers AI experts for cybersecurity, while debates arise over the risks and the suspension of the Mythos system.

title: Russia attacks Kyiv and damages historical monastery, killing nine
summary: Russian bombings affect the Ukrainian capital and a monastery declared a World Heritage Site.

title: Huge 150-meter industrial hose appears on beach in Japan
summary: Authorities search for the owner of the 300-ton hose stranded on the coast, which will be removed.

title: Japanese government reviews flag desecration law to exclude social media posts
summary: The proposed amendment seeks to respond to opposition criticism regarding freedom of expression.

title: Former Giants manager Shinnosuke Abe will not be prosecuted for assault
summary: Prosecutors decide not to charge Abe after considering the circumstances of the incident with his daughter.

title: Ormuz crisis impacts fertilizer and food prices in Japan
summary: Inflation could extend until next year due to the increase in raw materials and petroleum products.

summary: Authorities issue flood warnings in Okinawa and Kagoshima, and warn about the increase in extreme phenomena.

title: Wave of homicides in Japan: several murders shock the country
summary: Three murder cases in Osaka, Nagoya and other locations keep authorities on alert.

title: Political controversies in Japan: from controversial statements to embezzlement of funds
summary: Various scandals shake the political scene, with accusations of misuse of funds and statements that generate debate.

summary: After the success of the test mission, the new rocket will carry satellites on its next flight.

title: Fox acquires Roku for 3.5 trillion yen
summary: The US media giant buys the streaming platform to expand its digital presence.

title: Japanese university exempts fawn born on campus from entrance exam
summary: The university president announces that the fawn will not need to take the entrance exam, in a viral decision.

title: Fake police scams double in Japan
summary: Economic losses from these scams reach record numbers, and authorities recommend caution.

title: JR Tokai launches Disney-themed bullet train
summary: The new high-speed train service decorated with Disney characters will begin operating from Friday.
